skill.name: observability-engineer
skill.description: Build production-ready monitoring, logging, and tracing systems. Implements comprehensive observability strategies, SLI/SLO management, and incident response workflows.
skill.summary: Expert observability engineer for building monitoring, logging, tracing, and production reliability systems.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides observability engineering guidance through text prompts only. No dangerous patterns, no network requests, no file system access, and no external commands detected. Content describes legitimate monitoring, logging, and tracing system design.

content.value_statement: This skill helps you design and implement comprehensive monitoring, logging, and tracing systems for enterprise applications. It provides expert guidance on SLI/SLO management, distributed tracing, and incident response workflows.
actual_capabilities: Design comprehensive monitoring strategies with Prometheus, Grafana, and cloud-native tools
Implement distributed tracing with Jaeger, Zipkin, and OpenTelemetry
Define and track SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets for reliability management
Build alerting systems with PagerDuty, Slack, and intelligent noise reduction
Create observability pipelines with OpenTelemetry collector and multi-backend export
Implement log management with ELK Stack, Loki, and centralized logging
limitations: Cannot access actual metrics, logs, or tracing data - only provides guidance
Cannot execute infrastructure changes or deploy monitoring components
Requires access to observability tools and data sources for full effectiveness
Does not replace human incident response - augments decision making

use_cases.title: Design Microservices Monitoring Architecture
use_cases.description: Create a comprehensive monitoring strategy for a microservices system with 50+ services, including metrics collection, distributed tracing, and alerting.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and SREs building cloud-native applications

use_cases.title: Establish SLI/SLO Framework
use_cases.description: Define service level indicators, objectives, and error budgets for API services with 99.9% availability targets and burn rate monitoring.
use_cases.target_user: Site reliability engineers and platform teams

use_cases.title: Implement Distributed Tracing
use_cases.description: Set up distributed tracing for an e-commerce platform to identify latency bottlenecks and perform root cause analysis across service boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Monitoring Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a monitoring strategy for a [service type] that handles [traffic volume] requests per day. Include metrics collection, logging approach, and alerting recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with observability for a new service

prompt_templates.title: SLI/SLO Definition
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define SLIs and SLOs for our [service name] API with [availability target]% availability. Include error budget calculation and burn rate alerts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Establishing reliability targets for a production service

prompt_templates.title: Incident Response Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an incident response workflow for [incident type] including alert routing, escalation procedures, runbook recommendations, and post-incident analysis process.

prompt_templates.title: Cost Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze our current observability setup and recommend cost optimization strategies. We currently use [tools] and generate [volume] of telemetry data daily.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reducing observability costs while maintaining coverage

output_examples.input: Design a monitoring strategy for our checkout service handling 100K orders daily
output: Metrics Collection: Deploy Prometheus with recording rules for order throughput, latency percentiles (p50, p95, p99), and error rates by type
Key Dashboards: Create executive overview, operational real-time, and troubleshooting drill-down views in Grafana
Alerting: Configure alerts for p99 latency > 2s, error rate > 1%, and checkout success rate < 99%
Tracing: Implement OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation with trace sampling at 10% and full tracing for errors
Logging: Structured JSON logs with order ID, user ID, and latency for correlation with traces

output_examples.input: Define SLOs for our payment API with 99.9% availability target
output: SLI Definition: Successful payment requests / total payment requests, measured over 5-minute windows
SLO: 99.9% success rate over 30-day rolling window = 43.8 minutes allowed error budget
Error Budget Alerts: Burn rate alert at 2x (87.6 min/day) and 10x (438 min/day) thresholds
Consumption Tracking: Dashboard showing error budget remaining, daily burn rate, and projected breach date

best_practices: Start with business outcomes - define what reliable service means to users before choosing metrics
Implement progressive instrumentation: metrics first for visibility, then traces for debugging, then logs for detail
Alert on symptoms, not causes - notify when users are impacted, not when internal components fail
anti_patterns: Creating alerts for every possible failure - leads to alert fatigue and ignored notifications
Monitoring everything without purpose - increases cost and reduces signal quality
Setting SLOs too tightly - causes unnecessary stress and budget burnout

faq.question: What tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers major observability tools including Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger, Zipkin, ELK Stack, Loki, DataDog, New Relic, CloudWatch, OpenTelemetry, PagerDuty, and cloud-native monitoring across AWS, Azure, and GCP.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy monitoring infrastructure?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides design guidance, configuration recommendations, and implementation plans. Actual deployment requires separate infrastructure tools like Terraform or Kubernetes.

faq.question: How do I get started with observability?
faq.answer: Start by identifying your critical user journeys and defining what reliable service means. Then instrument for the golden signals: latency, traffic, errors, and saturation. Add traces and logs incrementally.

faq.question: What's the difference between monitoring and observability?
faq.answer: Monitoring tells you when something is wrong. Observability helps you understand why. Use metrics and dashboards for monitoring, traces for debugging, and logs for deep investigation.

faq.question: How do I reduce alert noise?
faq.answer: Use alert grouping, deduplication, and suppression rules. Alert on user-impacting symptoms rather than internal component failures. Implement runbooks for each alert to enable quick triage.

faq.question: What are SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets?
faq.answer: SLIs measure your service behavior (e.g., request success rate). SLOs are your target SLI values (e.g., 99.9% success). Error budgets are the allowed failure time remaining. Together they enable data-driven reliability decisions.
